item.title: Daily Briefing - 2026-04-12
item.description:
**Morning Briefing**
**What Changed Since Yesterday** Global markets saw mixed signals overnight. U.S. futures are slightly up. European indices largely flat. Oil prices advanced, reflecting supply concerns. Inflation data releases are due later this week.
**Venezuela Update** The political landscape remains tense. Opposition leaders call for increased protests against the regime. There are reports of further humanitarian aid being blocked. The international community is monitoring for any shifts in government policy.
**UK Response Signals** The UK government is considering additional sanctions against Venezuelan officials. Officials emphasize support for democratic processes in Venezuela. There remains a heightened focus on diplomatic engagement.
**Legal/Docket Watch** No significant updates in Epstein-related cases.
**What to Watch Today** 1. Watch for the release of U.S. inflation data. 2. Monitor developments from Venezuela, especially regarding protests. 3. Keep an eye on UK Parliament discussions around sanctions. 4. Observe market reactions to the inflation data and oil price fluctuations.
End of briefing.
